counting_method: deduped by message.id (global); one billable row per unique API response

recovered_session.reason: deleted from ~/.claude/projects between enumeration passes (live dir)
recovered_session.method: usage-signature dedup from first snapshot; validated == message.id dedup exactly on all present files

divergence.title: feat(blog): backfill launch-window posts, stage new
chore(blog): drop the Jul 3 post to the drawer
feat(blog): pre-write Jul 6 + Jul 8 posts as staged

notes: cwd-filtered from ~/.claude/projects by first cwd occurrence anywhere in file (line 1 is a queue-op record with no cwd; the task's head -1 snippet misses top-level sessions).

COUNT PER message.id, not per line: each API response is written to the transcript once per streamed content block (all sharing id + identical usage). Per-line counting inflates ~2.3x here (and ~13x historically).
Global dedup also collapses resume/fork-copied lines that retain original message.ids across files.
Manifest = top-level sessions + nested subagent/workflow transcripts. Subagents share the parent sessionId, so 'by chat' rolls subagent spend into its parent session.
Cache writes split into 5m (1.25x) and 1h (2.0x) TTL for precise costing per oc-claude-api/prompt-caching.md.
main + no-PR worktree branches surfaced as 'unattributed' (guardrail: don't force-fit).
